perturbation.original_text: In addition to capital recruitment functions, TKCI shall perform all of the functions and accept all of the responsibilities of the position of Chief Financial Officer including but not limited to management of accounts receivable and accounts payable, interfacing with Holding Company accountants on tax matters, interfacing with Holding Company accountants and lawyers on regulatory and compliance matters, and all other tasks typically and reasonably associated with the post and position of Chief Financial Officer.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to capital recruitment functions, TKCI shall perform some of the functions and accept some of the responsibilities of the position of Chief Financial Officer including but not limited to management of accounts receivable and accounts payable, interfacing with Holding Company accountants on tax matters, interfacing with Holding Company accountants and lawyers on regulatory and compliance matters, and all other tasks typically and reasonably associated with the post and position of Financial Consultant.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Chief Financial Officer' to 'Financial Consultant' and 'all of the functions and accept all of the responsibilities' to 'some of the functions and accept some of the responsibilities' creates an ambiguity. It is now unclear what specific responsibilities TKCI is obligated to fulfill, contradicting later sections that rely on TKCI fulfilling all duties of a CFO. This also introduces an issue of how will the unassigned functions will be executed within the company, and how will it be assigned to another party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to capital recruitment functions, TKCI shall perform some of the functions and accept some of the responsibilities of the position of Chief Financial Officer including but not limited to management of accounts receivable and accounts payable, interfacing with Holding Company accountants on tax matters, interfacing with Holding Company accountants and lawyers on regulatory and compliance matters, and all other tasks typically and reasonably associated with the post and position of Financial Consultant.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens TKCI's responsibilities from 'all' to 'some', which contradicts later sections that expect TKCI to fulfill all CFO duties. The change from 'Chief Financial Officer' to 'Financial Consultant' further exacerbates this contradiction, making it unclear what specific obligations TKCI is bound to, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability regarding the fulfillment of CFO responsibilities. The contradicted text highlights the altered and weakened responsibilities of TKCI.

perturbation.original_text: SIC, represented by Chief James E. Billie and Craig Talesman, will assist in the closing of the transactions by: 1) participating in meetings with key decision makers, providing process overview, and describing case histories of similarly situated projects; and 2) providing advice on issues such as i) location; (ii) deal structure; (iii) funding; (iv) timing/phasing; (v) cash flow/revenue collection issues; and (vi) other major implementation issues.
perturbation.changed_text: SIC, represented by Chief James E. Billie and Craig Talesman, may assist in the closing of the transactions by: 1) participating in meetings with key decision makers, providing process overview, and describing case histories of similarly situated projects; and 2) providing advice on issues such as i) location; (ii) deal structure; (iii) funding; (iv) timing/phasing; (v) cash flow/revenue collection issues; and (vi) other major implementation issues.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will assist' to 'may assist' transforms a mandatory obligation into a discretionary action. The impact is that SIC's assistance, previously considered a firm commitment, is now subject to their discretion. This creates uncertainty regarding their responsibilities in transaction closures, potentially leading to conflicts if their involvement is critical for project success.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SIC, represented by Chief James E. Billie and Craig Talesman, will assist in the finalization of project structuring plans geared toward maximizing all available revenue streams.
perturbation.justification: The original text states SIC 'will assist', creating an obligation. The changed text uses 'may assist', making the assistance discretionary. This directly contradicts the 'will assist' in the contradicted text, which assumes a firm commitment from SIC in finalizing project structuring plans and maximizing revenue streams. The change introduces uncertainty and a potential failure to fulfill the finalization of project structuring plans if SIC chooses not to assist, directly conflicting with the contradicted text's assumption of mandatory assistance.

perturbation.original_text: Net revenue from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company equally - 50/50 - to TKCI and SIC:
perturbation.changed_text: Net profit from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company as deemed appropiate - to TKCI and SIC:
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Net revenue' with 'Net profit' and the specific 50/50 split of revenue with a distribution 'as deemed appropriate' creates ambiguity. The original text is clearly define a distribution plan of revenues for both parties, now with the perturbation being introduced, the revenues distribution now lacks clarity, this creating uncertainty on how each party will be paid, depending on the net profit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Net profit from business operations created by Holding Company for the alliance will be distributed by Holding Company as deemed appropiate - to TKCI and SIC: SIC's original business concepts and plans, as well as opportunities brought to the table through its connections, and third-party contracts, are ways that we anticipate business could be generated, and revenues created; TKCI's advisory and management services and capital resources will provide the critical structure and business mechanism to carry concepts through to revenue.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Net revenue' with 'Net profit' and removes the specific 50/50 distribution, stating the distribution will be 'as deemed appropriate'. This directly contradicts the original intention of the contract, which was to provide a clear distribution plan. Furthermore, the changed text makes the contradicted text contradictory to itself. The first part of the contradicted text now lacks clarity because of the change, thus it causes ambiguity on how each party will be paid. The second part of the contradicted text states that SIC and TKCI will create revenue, but with the perturbation, there is no definition of how the revenue will be distributed. This meets the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
